kernel.name: Two-phase articulated plus free-rigid matrix-free GS solve

launch.granularity: one warp (32 threads) per world; dense articulated phase followed by a free-rigid MF phase

global_reads.name: world_constraint_count/world_dof_start
global_reads.notes: Sizes the dense articulated phase and locates the world velocity slice.

global_reads.name: J_world/Y_world
global_reads.notes: World-indexed articulated Jacobian rows and H^-1 J^T rows. Scenario bytes: g1_flat 50176 B combined, h1_tabletop 136192 B combined.

global_reads.name: rhs/diag/world_impulses
global_reads.notes: Articulated row bias, diagonal, and impulse state. rhs omits baked J*v_hat in matrix-free mode.

global_reads.name: mf_meta_packed
global_reads.notes: 128-bit packed free-rigid metadata with dof offsets, eff_mass_inv, rhs, and row projection metadata. Scenario bytes: 8192 B.

global_reads.name: mf_J_a/mf_J_b/mf_MiJt_a/mf_MiJt_b
global_reads.notes: Free-rigid Jacobians and H^-1 J^T vectors streamed during the second phase.

global_reads.name: mf_impulses
global_reads.notes: Warm-start state for the free-rigid rows.

global_writes.name: v_out
global_writes.notes: The live velocity vector is written back after the combined articulated and MF sweep.

global_writes.name: world_impulses/mf_impulses
global_writes.notes: Final impulse vectors for both phases.

shared_allocations.name: s_v
shared_allocations.notes: World velocity vector lives in shared memory across both phases of the solve.

shared_allocations.name: s_lam_dense/s_rhs_dense/s_diag_dense/s_rtype_dense/s_parent_dense/s_mu_dense
shared_allocations.notes: Articulated row state and metadata preloaded before the dense phase.

shared_allocations.name: s_lam_mf
shared_allocations.notes: Free-rigid impulse state kept in shared memory for the second phase.

register_resident.name: prefetched J/Y lane slices
register_resident.notes: Software-pipeline registers for the current and next articulated row.

register_resident.name: packed metadata fields
register_resident.notes: Decoded from mf_meta_packed for the free-rigid branch.

streamed_inputs: Articulated rows stream J_world and Y_world row slices instead of a preassembled Delassus matrix.
The free-rigid phase streams mf_J_* and mf_MiJt_* plus packed metadata from global memory.
preloaded_inputs: The live world velocity vector v_out is preloaded into shared memory as s_v and reused across all iterations.
Dense articulated rhs, diag, impulses, and row metadata are staged into shared memory once per world.
Free-rigid impulses are staged into shared memory once; metadata is compressed into mf_meta_packed for coalesced loads.
recomputed_values: Every articulated row recomputes J_i * v from the live shared velocity vector instead of reading C_i:.
Every free-rigid row recomputes J_i * v from the current shared body/world velocity state.
Only the diagonal C_ii is materialized explicitly for articulated rows; off-diagonal couplings are reconstructed on the fly through J and Y.
dominant_work: Articulated phase: evaluate w_i = rhs_i + J_i * v, project lambda_i, then apply v += Y_i * delta_lambda_i.
Free-rigid phase: evaluate w_i = mf_rhs_i + J_i * v, project lambda_i, then apply v += MiJt_i * delta_lambda_i.
One shared velocity vector couples the articulated and free-rigid phases without assembling a dense mixed-world C matrix.

observations: In g1_flat the matrix-free preset still allocates the larger articulated buffers (25088 B each for J_world and Y_world) but gains no free-rigid rows, so it is mostly a control case for the docs.
In h1_tabletop the matrix-free path routes 102 rows through the free-rigid branch and leaves only 30 articulated rows in the dense articulated phase.
For h1_tabletop the articulated matrix-free storage is dominated by J_world + Y_world (136192 B), while the free-rigid branch adds 63488 B.
This kernel is the main explanation target for the surprising result: it does not need a shared-memory copy of a dense Delassus matrix, only the velocity vector and scalar row state.
